title: Kraunhia brachybotrys
taxonomy: Kraunhia brachybotrys is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Kraunhia and is native to the Americas. The family Orchidaceae, to which Kraunhia brachybotrys belongs, is one of the largest and most diverse plant families in the world. The species Kraunhia brachybotrys is a type of terrestrial orchid, which grows in the ground. Further classification of the species within the Orchidaceae family is needed for more precise information.

intro: Kraunhia brachybotrys, also known as Korean angelica or Brachybotrys, is a type of flowering plant in the family Compositae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from fever, rheumatism, and menstrual disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic effects. The bioactive constituents of Kraunhia brachybotrys include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic benefits. In traditional Korean medicine, Kraunhia brachybotrys is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in combination with other herbs in formulas to treat various health conditions.
